seoTitle: How to Join a Channel in Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Browse and join public Slack channels using the workspace directory, preview messages first, and organize new channels in your sidebar.
intro: Staying connected to the right project rooms and department updates gets harder as a Slack workspace grows. This guide covers browsing the channel directory, previewing a room before joining, and organizing new channels in your sidebar.

steps.heading: Open the channel browser
steps.body: Click 'More' or the Channels header in the left sidebar to reveal the exploration menu.

steps.heading: Browse the workspace directory
steps.body: Select 'Browse Channels' to open the full list of public channels available in your workspace.

steps.heading: Search by keyword
steps.body: Type a topic keyword into the search bar to filter the list down to relevant channels.

steps.heading: Preview before joining
steps.body: Click a channel name to view its recent messages and shared files before deciding to join.

steps.heading: Join the channel
steps.body: Click the 'Join Channel' button and confirm the room now appears in your sidebar.

faq.question: Can I join a private channel the same way?
faq.answer: No, private channels require an invite from an existing member since they don't appear in the public directory.

faq.question: How do I leave a channel later?
faq.answer: Open the channel, click its name at the top, and select 'Leave channel' from the details menu.

faq.question: Can I organize joined channels into custom sections?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag channels into custom sidebar sections to keep your workspace organized.

faq.question: Will I see message history from before I joined?
faq.answer: Yes, in most workspaces new members can scroll back through a public channel's full history.
